name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 10.11.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 10.11.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Rabi  Sowing  Status: Rabi  sowing  data  collection  has  been  started  and  the  sowing  is  in preliminary stage. As on November 07, 2025, the area sown under Rabi crops is 130.32  lakh ha (including 53.35 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard) as compared to 102.32 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rabi  Sowing  Status: Rabi  sowing  data  collection  has  been  started  and  the  sowing  is  in preliminary stage. As on November 07, 2025, the area sown under Rabi crops is 130.32  lakh ha (including 53.35 lakh ha in Rapeseed & Mustard) as compared to 102.32 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · As on 04.11.2025,  43.25%  of  area  has  been  harvested  all  over  India.  Approximately  53.00% area of Pulses mainly in Urdbean (73%), Moongbean (96%) & Mothbean (100%); 83.91% area of Oilseeds majorly in Groundnut (70.70%) & Sesamum (75.04%); 64.13% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals majorly in Jowar (73.80%)  & Bajra (89.13%) and 30% area of Rice has been harvested for kharif crops 2025-26.
texts.text: As on 04.11.2025,  43.25%  of  area  has  been  harvested  all  over  India.  Approximately  53.00% area of Pulses mainly in Urdbean (73%), Moongbean (96%) & Mothbean (100%); 83.91% area of Oilseeds majorly in Groundnut (70.70%) & Sesamum (75.04%); 64.13% area of Shri Anna cum Coarse Cereals majorly in Jowar (73.80%)  & Bajra (89.13%) and 30% area of Rice has been harvested for kharif crops 2025-26.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Minimum temperature is likely to be below normal in most of India except in NE India and some parts of J&K.
texts.text: Temperature: Minimum temperature is likely to be below normal in most of India except in NE India and some parts of J&K.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall  Distribution: From  October  01 -November  10  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall  is  37%  higher  than  its  Long  Period Average  (LPA),  with regional variations:  (i) East  &  Northeast  India:  16%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  128%;  (iii)  Central  India:  63%;  and South Peninsula: 16%.
texts.text: Rainfall  Distribution: From  October  01 -November  10  2025,  the  All-India  cumulative rainfall  is  37%  higher  than  its  Long  Period Average  (LPA),  with regional variations:  (i) East  &  Northeast  India:  16%;  (ii)  Northwest  India:  128%;  (iii)  Central  India:  63%;  and South Peninsula: 16%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Light  to  moderate  rainfall/thunderstorm  at  most/many  places  with  isolated  heavy rainfall very likely over Tamil Nadu during 12th & 13th, and over Kerala & Mahe on 10th November.
texts.text: Light  to  moderate  rainfall/thunderstorm  at  most/many  places  with  isolated  heavy rainfall very likely over Tamil Nadu during 12th & 13th, and over Kerala & Mahe on 10th November.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Thunderstorm  with  lightning  very  likely  over  Tamil  Nadu  during  10th-13th  and  over Kerala & Mahe on 10th November.
texts.text: Thunderstorm  with  lightning  very  likely  over  Tamil  Nadu  during  10th-13th  and  over Kerala & Mahe on 10th November.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Isolated to scattered rainfall activity likely over Kerala & Mahe, Tamil Nadu, Andaman & Nicobar Islands.
texts.text: Isolated to scattered rainfall activity likely over Kerala & Mahe, Tamil Nadu, Andaman & Nicobar Islands.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · No significant weather likely over rest parts of the country during the week.
texts.text: No significant weather likely over rest parts of the country during the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall, the Northeast Monsoon is likely to be in weak phase over South Peninsular India during the week.
texts.text: Overall, the Northeast Monsoon is likely to be in weak phase over South Peninsular India during the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  06  November  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  165.278  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  90.57%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (182.479  BCM).  The  current  year's storage is nearly 105.60 percent of last year's storage and  118.69 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  06  November  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  165.278  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  90.57%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (182.479  BCM).  The  current  year's storage is nearly 105.60 percent of last year's storage and  118.69 percent of the average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater  Levels :  In  August  2025,  around  89%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.
texts.text: Groundwater  Levels :  In  August  2025,  around  89%  of  the  country's  monitoring  stations exhibited a depth to water level within 10 meters below ground level. Deeper water levels of more than 20 m are observed in around 5% of stations of the country covering mainly the western states, especially Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during August 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.
texts.text: The deeper groundwater level in the states like Western Rajasthan, Punjab, Haryana and Chandigarh during August 2025 has been significantly influenced by the over extraction of groundwater compared to the recharge during the monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Annual water level comparison with the previous year August 2024 to August 2025 has shown  that  about  56.5%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country  experienced  rise  in annual water level fluctuation because of the higher rainfall in 2025 compared to 2024 in Monsoon period.
texts.text: Annual water level comparison with the previous year August 2024 to August 2025 has shown  that  about  56.5%  of  total  analyzed  stations  of  the  country  experienced  rise  in annual water level fluctuation because of the higher rainfall in 2025 compared to 2024 in Monsoon period.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  August 2025.
texts.text: About 70% of the analyzed stations of the unconfined aquifer experienced a rise of water level  in  the  decadal  mean  water  level  fluctuation  of  2015-2024  with  respect  to  August 2025.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.
texts.text: Pests  &  Diseases :  There  is  no  major  pest  incidence  that  has  been  reported.  The  overall intensity of pests and diseases remained below the Economic Threshold Level.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · During the week (30 Oct -05 Nov 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 93%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 05 Nov 2025) is higher than LPA by 45%.
texts.text: During the week (30 Oct -05 Nov 2025), lower rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 93%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st Oct to 05 Nov 2025) is higher than LPA by 45%.

texts.text: Week 1 (07 Nov -13 Nov 2025):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · There  is  no  probability  of  cold  wave  conditions  over  any  part  of  the  country  during  the week.
texts.text: There  is  no  probability  of  cold  wave  conditions  over  any  part  of  the  country  during  the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overall,  above  normal  minimum temperatures are likely to prevail over Northeast India, and some parts of the western Himalayan region during the week.
texts.text: Overall,  above  normal  minimum temperatures are likely to prevail over Northeast India, and some parts of the western Himalayan region during the week.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Week 2 (14 Nov -20 Nov 2025):
texts.text: Week 2 (14 Nov -20 Nov 2025):

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Minimum temperatures are likely to be near normal or slightly above normal over northeast India and many parts of Western Himalayan Region. These are below normal by 2-4 0  C over remaining parts of the country except east India, where these are likely to be below normal by 3-5 0  C during most days of the week.
texts.text: Minimum temperatures are likely to be near normal or slightly above normal over northeast India and many parts of Western Himalayan Region. These are below normal by 2-4 0  C over remaining parts of the country except east India, where these are likely to be below normal by 3-5 0  C during most days of the week.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Soil moisture during 31 Oct - 06 Nov is similar/better than the average of the past 9 years in major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Gujarat,  Jharkhand,  Odisha, Haryana, Punjab, Karnataka, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra, Telangana, A.P. and Bihar.

texts.label: list_item

texts.text: Availability  of  fertilizer  has  been  found  comfortable.  Details  of  Requirement,  Receipt, Availability, Sales and Stock position of Major Fertilizers (Urea, DAP, MOP, Complex and SSP) during Rabi 2025-26 as on 07.11.2025 is given below:

texts.text: The  above  data  indicates  that  the  availability  of  Urea,  MOP,  Complexes  and  SSP  is comfortable.
